title: ranunculus pedatifidus subsp. arcticus
intro: Ranunculus pedatifidus subsp. arcticus, also known as the Arctic Buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Ranunculus pedatifidus subsp. arcticus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional folk remedies to treat a range of ailments.
